issues: key height approximately 90px in 672px viewport; with 32x32 player at ~54px screen height, key approaches or slightly exceeds player size, violating items-never-larger-than-player rule
no player sprite directly visible in scene for precise comparison
key is shown in hub/UI overlay context rather than in-world, making size judgment uncertain
reasoning: The key_gold_v02 asset appears in a dungeon hub UI panel between the '回遗迹前奏' label and an arrow pointing to a locked Boss door. Estimating from viewport height (672px) and assuming a ~400 game-unit tall view, 1 game unit ≈ 1.68 screen px, placing a 32px player at ~54 screen px. The key sprite reads roughly 85-90px tall on screen, suggesting ~50-53 game units — at or slightly above the player's 32-unit height. Item category 'key' should sit at 50-75% of player (16-24 units), so the asset is noticeably oversized for its category. Art style is consistent pixel art with flat 2D top-down perspective and good toy-diorama detailing; no perspective mismatch or scene-overwhelm issues. Size is the sole concern but it is a concrete rule violation. Scored marginal (6): pass requires clear sub-75% sizing relative to player.
